title: Distrust.
content:
To safeguard man from wrongs, there nothing must
    Be truer to him than a wise distrust.
    And to thyself be best this sentence known:
    Hear all men speak, but credit few or none.
meaning:
Robert Herrick's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Distrust."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
